summary: Iteration 5 (posterUrl field across stack + MediaCard artwork) fully verified. Backend pytest 43/43 green — added TestPosterUrl (7 tests covering null default, valid http(s), empty='clear', invalid 422, update-clear via '', update-invalid 422, update-set new) and TestWestwoodRanchPosters (2 tests: all 9 seeded items have unsplash https posters; backfill idempotent — after iter4's reseed-twice block, count still 9 and 0 missing). MediaCreate/MediaUpdate posterUrl validator behaves correctly: optional, max 2048, must start with http(s), '' on update -> None ($set: null). _media_to_dict surfaces posterUrl in both list and create/update responses; legacy docs (no posterUrl key) still return posterUrl=null via doc.get(). Frontend Playwright verified all review-request items: (1) MediaForm has [data-testid=media-poster-url-input], invalid value triggers inline [data-testid=media-poster-error] 'Must start with http:// or https://'; (2) YouTube helper button [data-testid=media-poster-use-youtube] appears only when sourceType='embed' + a YT URL is parsed (verified: visible with watch?v=ABC123XYZ_0, hidden after switching sourceType to 'direct'); clicking it fills posterUrl with https://img.youtube.com/vi/ABC123XYZ_0/hqdefault.jpg as required; (3) Preview thumbnail [data-testid=media-poster-preview] and clear button [data-testid=media-poster-clear] render when posterUrl is non-empty; clear empties the field; (4) Creating a media item via the UI with posterUrl='https://images.unsplash.com/photo-test-poster' persists — subsequent GET reflects it; (5) Westwood Ranch profile shell shows count of [data-testid^=media-poster-] === 9 (img elements present in DOM regardless of network resolution success); (6) Source badge over poster carries bg-black/55 + backdrop-blur-md (asserted in element classList); (7) VideoPlayer
critical_code_review_comments: MediaCreate.posterUrl validator returns None on empty string (treated as cleared), but MediaUpdate.posterUrl validator returns '' (sentinel) and then update_media converts '' to None. The split is intentional but the comment in the validator says 'Treat empty string as clear the poster' — the magic-string contract between validator and route is fragile. Consider unifying: validator returns None on '' for both, and ALSO allow the route to detect explicit None vs unset via exclude_unset (already done). Right now `if 'posterUrl' in updates and updates['posterUrl'] == ''` is dead-code-safe but confusing.
Carry-over from iter 3/4: @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') is deprecated — migrate to FastAPI lifespan.

MediaCard hides the broken image via display:none; the surrounding wrapper is still 100% wide/tall but has bg-[#0a0a0d] so a dark wash shows. However the bottom-up gradient (rgba up to 0.92) ALSO renders even when img is hidden, making the visual band look almost fully dark for any 404'd poster. Consider also hiding the gradient overlay onError, or use an `imgLoaded` state and only render the gradient when the image loaded successfully. This is a polish item, not a bug.

extractYouTubeId(url) accepts ABC123XYZ_0 (length 11, mixed chars) — regex /^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{6,}$/ only checks the youtu.be branch. For the watch?v= branch there's NO regex sanity check — any value of `v` is accepted, including empty or arbitrary strings. Frontend will then generate a broken hqdefault URL. Low risk because the helper is opt-in, but a quick `if (!id || !/^[A-Za-z0-9_-]{8,15}$/.test(id)) return null;` would harden it.

Backend Pydantic posterUrl validator only checks http(s) prefix — it does NOT verify URL parseability. e.g. 'http://[' passes. Acceptable since FE uses which fails gracefully; document this in API contract.
test_credentials: Master admin passcode: 1115. Westwood Ranch profile passcode: 1976. Both confirmed against /app/memory/test_credentials.md.
seed_data_creation: No new seed data. Westwood Ranch profile (server-seeded) verified to have non-null https://images.unsplash.com/... posterUrl on all 9 media items. Tests create 1 TEST_PosterProfile (deleted in teardown) + a couple of TEST_-prefixed media items in Westwood Ranch (deleted inline after assertions).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 5 test ids in use: media-poster-url-input, media-poster-error, media-poster-use-youtube, media-poster-preview, media-poster-clear, media-poster-{id} (on MediaCard), player-video poster attribute. YouTube helper button visibility is gated by BOTH (sourceType==='embed') AND (extractYouTubeId(sourceUrl) returns non-null) AND (current posterUrl !== suggested URL). To bypass passcode dialog in Playwright tests: sessionStorage['mh_profile_'+id] = JSON.stringify(profilePublic) before navigating to /profile/{id}. The frontend MediaCard uses onError -> display:none, so the [data-testid='media-poster-*'] element is ALWAYS present in DOM regardless of whether the URL resolves; assert via locator.count() not visibility (some Unsplash IDs in the seed are speculative and return 404, as warned in the original problem statement). Backend asyncio.run(_seed_westwood_ranch()) approach used in iter4 TestSeedIdempotent works ONLY for the first invocation inside pytest — subsequent classes calling asyncio.run hit 'Event loop is closed' on motor; iter5 TestWestwoodRanchPosters.test_backfill_is_idempotent therefore relies on the iter4 reseed already having run and just re-checks the post-state via HTTP. Keep this ordering if adding more seed-related tests.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found in iteration 5. One internal test issue (motor 'Event loop is closed' on a 2nd asyncio.run) was resolved by replacing direct reseeding with HTTP post-state assertion, which is more representative of real-world idempotency anyway.
